Notice Of Motion And Motion To Continue Stay Of Trial Court Proceedings Pending Appellate Resolution
Off Calendar Oct-04-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TURO INC.'s Motion To Continue Stay Of Trial Court Proceedings Pending Appellate Resolution is granted. The parties previously stipulated to a continued stay of proceedings pending the California Supreme Court's decision on Turo's petition for review. Now that the Supreme Court has granted that petition and transferred the matter to the Court of Appeal, and the Court of Appeal has issued an order to show cause setting a briefing schedule, a continued stay pending the Court of Appeal's resolution of the central issue in the case-whether Turo constitutes a "rental car company" under California law-makes eminent sense. A continued stay would avoid the need for duplicative and possibly unnecessary discovery, motion practice, and trial, and would not prejudice the City, particularly in view of its unopposed motion for calendar preference. The stay shall expire on issuance of the remittitur.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Turo'S Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending Outcome Of Writ Petition
Off Calendar Jun-09-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Deputy City Atty. KRISTINE POPLAWSKI, Esq. of the Office of the City Attorney (
(Tel: 415-554-3831) appearing via CourtCall for plaintiff the People of the State of Californa;
Atty. BETHANY LOBO, Esq. of Cooley LLP (Tel: 415-693-2187) appearing via CourtCall for defendant Turo, Inc.

Hearing Re: Defendant Turo, Inc.'s Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending Outcome Of Writ Petition.

The matter is argued and submitted by counsel for both sides.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling adding some additional language as follows:

Defendant Turo Inc.'s motion to stay proceedings pending outcome of writ petition is granted. The Court of Appeal has issued a Palma notice and requested the City to file an opposition to Turo's petition for writ of mandate. As the City concedes, at least a partial stay is warranted to avoid the risk of duplicative trials. In the Court's view, a complete stay of the litigation, including discovery, for the relatively brief period of time necessary for the Court of Appeal to resolve the pending petition would further judicial economy, and would reduce burden and expense on the parties and the court. Either party may request that the stay be modified or lifted upon the showing of a change of circumstances.

The Court signs an order.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

(Re-Notice) Of Previously Filed Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs And Cross-Defendants Compliance Under California Code Of Civil Procedure 2031.320
Off Calendar Jul-31-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Deputy City Attys. KRISTINE A. POPLAWSKI and MARC PRICE WOLF of the San Francisco Office of the City Attorney (Tel: 415-554-3878) appearing for plaintiffs the People of the State of California and the City and County of San Francisco;
Attys. BETHANY LOBO, ESQ., ASHLEY KEMPER CORKERY, ESQ., and MAX BERNSTEIN, ESQ. of Cooley LLP (Tel: 415-693-2187) appearing for defendant Turo, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT TURO INC.'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs And Cross-Defendants Compliance Under California Code Of Civil Procedure 2031.320.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear to address the possibility of the Court appointing a Discovery Referee for this case. The Court indicates it will hold off on appointing a Discovery Referee at the present time but may revisit the issue at a later date. The motion is argued and submitted. DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Natalie Orr, Deputy City Attorney and Jaime Huling Delaye, Deputy City Attorney 415-554-3800 of the Office of the City Attorney.
Matthew Brown, Esq. 415-693-2188, Benjamin Kleine, Esq. 415-693-2022, Bethany Lobo, Esq.
415-693-2187 of Colley for Defendant Turo.
A representative from Turo is also present.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces the tentative rulings. Both sides argue. After extensive argument, (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) the Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA AND CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO TURO INC.'S CROSS COMPLAINT.

Taken Under Submission. The Court Overruled the Demurrer as to the first cause of action as to the City, Sustained without leave to amend the Demurrer as to all four causes of action in the Cross-complaint as to the People and will grant leave to amend once it rules on the taken under submission matters. The equitable Defenses are Taken under Submission. The Dormant Commerce Clause is also Taken Under submssion. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
